sources.name: Canada's National Artificial Intelligence Strategy: AI for All
sources.publisher: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED)
sources.citation: Government of Canada. (2026). Canada's National Artificial Intelligence Strategy: AI for All. ISED.

sources.name: Analysis on artificial intelligence use by businesses in Canada, Q2 2024
sources.citation: Bryan, V., Sood, S., & Johnston, C. (2024). Analysis on artificial intelligence use by businesses in Canada, second quarter of 2024. Statistics Canada Catalogue no. 11-621-M.

sources.name: CIFAR Impact Report 2023-2024
sources.citation: CIFAR. (2024). Impact Report 2023-2024. Canadian Institute for Advanced Research.

adoption.note: Statistics Canada Canadian Survey on Business Conditions, Q2 2024 (April-May 2024)
adoption.national_ai_adoption_definition: % of Canadian businesses using AI in producing goods or delivering services in the last 12 months
adoption.current_adoption_gap: Canada adopts AI at 12% broadly, well behind Nordic leaders at 29-42%
adoption.sme_note: 78% of non-adopting firms report barriers; SMEs (under 500 employees) represent 99.8% of Canadian businesses and employ 14.3 million workers — this is where Canada's adoption challenge lives

workforce.note: Sources: AI for All Strategy 2026, CIFAR Impact Report 2023-2024

employment_impact_of_adoption.note: 84.9% of AI-adopting businesses reported no change in employee numbers (Q2 2024)

pro_worker_evidence.note: OECD survey of manufacturing and finance workers across 7 countries including Canada: 80% said AI improved their performance; AI users 4x more likely to report gains in job satisfaction

research_talent.talent_context: Canada has successfully attracted international AI researchers through the Canada CIFAR AI Chairs program; 129 active chairs including international recruits. Canada ranks 2nd globally in AI research papers per capita.

investment.compute_access_fund: Enhance access to public compute for SMEs — specific amount not disclosed

adoption_gap.gap: Canada lags Nordic countries (29-42%) significantly; SME adoption is the core challenge

sovereignty.concern: Over-dependence on foreign AI platforms and compute infrastructure
sovereignty.context: Canada advantaged in research but lagging in adoption and over-exposed to foreign technology dependencies
sovereignty.response: Sovereign AI foundation pillar — Canadian compute, Canadian data infrastructure, Canadian AI champions

regional_disparity.gap: AI benefits concentrated in major urban centres; rural, remote, and Indigenous communities face access barriers
regional_disparity.indigenous_note: Considerable evidence of disproportionate exposure to AI harms for Indigenous Peoples; Canada committed to supporting Indigenous leadership in AI governance

labour_displacement.current_evidence: 84.9% of adopting businesses report no employment change (Q2 2024)
labour_displacement.concern: Rapid scaling from 6.1% to 60% adoption by 2034 would represent major structural shift
labour_displacement.pro_worker_approach: Government strategy explicitly commits to augmentation over displacement; OECD evidence shows positive outcomes when implemented pro-worker

sme_capacity.barriers: cost, skills gaps, lack of trusted implementation pathways, compliance burden

regulatory_context.eu_ai_act: Passed 2024 — Canada has no equivalent binding legislation
regulatory_context.canada_directives: Treasury Board Directive on Automated Decision-Making (2019, updated 2023) — applies only to federal government
regulatory_context.bill_c27_aida: Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA) — proposed but not yet passed as of 2026
regulatory_context.accountability_gap: No binding AI disclosure or accountability requirements for private sector in Canada
regulatory_context.global_context: UK, EU, US all developing AI governance frameworks; Canada risks regulatory gap creating competitive and trust disadvantage

ai_disclosure_accountability.key_tension: Disclosure requirement creates compliance burden for 99.8% of SMEs to regulate practices that only 6.1% currently use
ai_disclosure_accountability.workers_affected_estimate: 150,000 directly in AI roles; potentially millions more as adoption reaches 60% target

relevant_stats: Canada ranks 2nd in AI research per capita
ai_compute_investment.key_tension: Public compute investment benefits research community and large firms; SME access mechanisms unclear

ai_skills_training.key_tension: Urban concentration of AI institutes vs need for national skills distribution
